content.problemStatement:
Neca is a farmer in a village called Red River and he has NNN cows. iii-th cow produces aia_iai​ liters of milk and Neca collects the milk in nnn buckets of infinite size (one bucket for one cow). At the end of the day he wants to merge all the milk into one bucket. However, he wants to do it as fast as possible!

Since the buckets are infinite, they are magic as well - in order to merge two buckets with AAA and BBB liters of milk he needs A+BA+BA+B units of time.

Help Neca calculate the minimum amount of time he needs in order to merge the milk.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of input of every test case contains a single integer NNN
The second line of input of every test case contains NNN integers - a1,a2,...,ana_1, a_2, ..., a_na1​,a2​,...,an​


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line minimal amount of time that Neca needs in order to merge all the milk into one bucket.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N – the number of cows (buckets). The second line contains N space‑separated integers a1 a2 … aN, where ai is the amount of milk in the i‑th bucket.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimal total time required to merge all milk into one bucket.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: merge 1+1=2 (cost2), then 2+2=4 (cost4), then 4+1000=1004 (cost1004). Total=1010. Second test case: merge 1+1=2 (cost2), then 2+1=3 (cost3). Total=5.

solution.approach: Merging two buckets of sizes A and B costs A+B. This is exactly the classic optimal merging problem solved by Huffman's algorithm. Repeatedly merge the two smallest buckets, add their sum to the answer, and push the new bucket back. A min‑heap (priority queue) gives O(N log N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, where N is the number of buckets in that test case.

code.Python3:
import sys, heapq

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        heap = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        ans = 0
        while len(heap) > 1:
            a = heapq.heappop(heap)
            b = heapq.heappop(heap)
            s = a + b
            ans += s
            heapq.heappush(heap, s)
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
